items.title: 'It's about fairness': Australian Iranians hit back at 'racist' visa ban

children.content: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

items.title: Signs point to measles cases spreading undetected in NSW

children.content: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

image.altText: Gloved hands prepare a needle for vaccination.

items.title: Blueberry industry under scrutiny amid rapid growth

children.content: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

image.altText: Blueberries on a bush in greenhouse.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

gallery.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

items.title: Cotton and Davis involved in post-match spat after 36ers level NBL finals

children.content: Sydney coach Brian Goorjian insists he's not concerned by Kendric Davis's post-match tirade at Adelaide superstar Bryce Cotton, following the 36ers' NBL championship-levelling 91-89 win over the Kings.

image.altText: Tempers flare in the last quarter between Bryce Cotton of the Adelaide 36ers and Kendric Davis of the Sydney Kings

items.title: Socceroos snatch late win as teen debutant impresses heading into World Cup

children.content: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

image.altText: Socceroos player Jordy Bos has a finger pointed in the air and smiling as he celebrates a goal

items.title: Barnacle-covered rare turtle washed ashore makes full recovery

children.content: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

image.altText: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery

gallery.altText: A composite image showing a turtle covered in shells on its back on the sand and an inset pic of a turtle's face.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image showing a turtle covered in shells on its back on the sand and an inset pic of a turtle's face.

items.title: 'Forget it': NSW beachgoers urged to cancel plans amid hazardous weather

children.content: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

image.altText: A large wave with surfers and swimmers beyond it and houses seen in the distance.

items.title: Former premier Kristina Keneally reveals major regret of political life

children.content: The former NSW and Labor premier said she regrets not acting on developing Glebe Island for housing, but added the GFC made the task complicated.

image.altText: a woman talking into a micrphone looking to the side

items.title: Parts of NSW wake up to 'surreal' snowfall in March

children.content: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

gallery.altText: A man in a green coat and red pants standing in snow with a tree in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green coat and red pants standing in snow with a tree in the background.

items.title: Waratahs snap losing streak with upset win over Brumbies in Canberra

children.content: NSW Waratahs coach Dan McKellar heralds his side's much-needed 30-28 victory against bitter rivals the ACT Brumbies as a special memory for the club.

image.altText: Andrew Kellaway of the Waratahs celebrates with teammates after scoring a try

items.title: Manly sacks coach Seibold after three games of 2026 season

children.content: Manly has sacked head coach Anthony Seibold after a dismal start to his fourth NRL season at the helm.

image.altText: A coach looks on before a rugby league match

items.title: Balcony identified as likely mould infection site linked to patient deaths

children.content: A balcony at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital has been identified as the infection site of a mould cluster linked to two patient deaths late last year.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of construction works red arrow pointing to top of a building

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of construction works red arrow pointing to top of a building

items.title: Assault charges against Wendell Sailor dismissed on mental health grounds

children.content: All charges are dropped against former footballer Wendell Sailor, who is discharged into his doctor's care.

image.altText: Man in dark suit, pink shirt and grey hat

items.title: Inmate not checked for 21 hours before 'preventable' death, inquest told

children.content: The coronial inquest hears Dictor Dongrin, 29, was not assessed for 21 hours at Clarence Correctional Centre after he was admitted with alcohol withdrawal.

image.altText: Aerial shot of a newly-built prison surrounded by pastoral land.

items.title: 'Can't ring my family': War reshapes ancient festival for Iranian Australians

children.content: As Iranian Australians try to celebrate Persian new year, news of the conflict in the Middle East is always front of mind.

gallery.altText: Leila looking at the camera with a backdrop of cultural artworks in the background at Wagga's library.
Local Iranian artist from Wagga Wagga performances traditional dance for Iranian New Year.
Eishan Ensenmble perform at Nowruz concert in Wagga Wagga.

thumbnail.altText: Leila looking at the camera with a backdrop of cultural artworks in the background at Wagga's library.
Local Iranian artist from Wagga Wagga performances traditional dance for Iranian New Year.
Eishan Ensenmble perform at Nowruz concert in Wagga Wagga.

items.title: Jailed ex-Labor MP plans to use legal aid to appeal sex abuse convictions

children.content: Paedophile and former NSW Labor politician Milton Orkopoulos says he has Legal Aid funding to explore an appeal against his convictions for abusing seven boys.

image.altText: An older man wearing a shirt

items.title: Broncos claim pressure-relieving victory over Dolphins, ending tough week on a high

children.content: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

image.altText: Kotoni Staggs dives with the ball

items.title: 'I've got a family to support': Sandilands's battle to stay on air in court

children.content: Kyle Sandilands says he wants to "get back to work as quick as possible" as a legal battle between the controversial radio host and his former employer reaches court for the first time.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands has white hair and a beard and is surrounded by reporters.

items.title: Second 'crew' charged over Sydney woman's kidnapping, murder

children.content: Further charges have been laid over the alleged kidnapping and murder of a western Sydney woman who police believe was targeted due to her husband's organised crime links.

image.altText: Thi Kim Tran in a selfie photo, with a purple background, smiling.

items.title: Video captures dramatic moment crane collapses in powerful storm

children.content: The crane was buffeted by a powerful storm before the boom came loose and fell onto a nearby apartment building.

image.altText: Crane collapsing into building amid a powerful storm

gallery.altText: A crane bent over near a building, with a grey sky in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A crane bent over near a building, with a grey sky in the background.

items.title: 'Electrons to the people:' Neighbours build commercial solar farm

children.content: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

image.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

items.title: Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale a reminder of Manly's past amid an uncertain future

children.content: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

image.altText: Daly Cherry-Evans shakes hands with a Manly player
